content:
{
  "title": "Counterfactual Prompt Augmentation to Mitigate Social Bias in Language Models",
  "problem": "Large language models often exhibit social biases and stereotypes when generating responses, even with careful prompt design. Existing debiasing techniques focus on model fine-tuning or post-processing, leaving prompt-based methods underexplored.",
  "motivation": "Prompting is a non-invasive and broadly applicable method to influence model behavior, crucial as LLMs are increasingly accessed via APIs with limited retraining capabilities. Addressing biases at the prompt level offers scalable debiasing without requiring access to model internals. Novel prompting strategies could provide practical mitigation of harmful stereotypes at inference time.",
  "method": "We propose Counterfactual Prompt Augmentation (CPA), where each input prompt is paired with minimally edited counterfactual versions that change key demographic variables (e.g., gender, ethnicity, age). The LLM is prompted to answer both the original and counterfactual prompts, followed by a meta-prompt that instructs the model to reflect on and revise its initial response if it identifies stereotype-driven disparities. CPA can be implemented as a wrapper that chains and structures prompts, requiring no model changes.",
  "experiment_plan": "Experiments will use social bias benchmark datasets such as StereoSet and CrowS-Pairs, comparing CPA against standard prompting and prior debiasing prompt techniques. Metrics will include bias scores (e.g., stereotypical association measurements) and answer utility/hallucination rates. Human evaluation will verify stereotype reduction and response quality."
}
